guest.script:
EXT. MOUNTAIN COMPLEX - DAWN

The first rays of sunlight paint the rugged peaks in gold. Below, nestled precariously on a sheer cliff face, is a high-tech facility, carved into the rock.

INT. SERVER ROOM - CONTINUOUS

ALEX (30s, sharp, determined) works feverishly at a console. Sweat beads on his forehead. Wires snake everywhere. His fingers fly, hitting ENTER.

A small DATA DRIVE ejects with a soft WHIRR. Alex snatches it. Holds it tight. This is it.

The room is silent for a beat. Then —

A high-pitched ALARM rips through the air. Red lights begin to flash.

Alex’s eyes narrow. He was quick, but not quick enough.

He shoves the drive into his tactical vest, pulls out a silenced PISTOL. Moves.

INT. MOUNTAIN COMPLEX CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS

Alex sprints down a polished corridor. The alarm SCREAMS. Doors LOCK down with heavy THUDS.

He slides to a halt, seeing a reinforced steel door block his path. No escape this way.

FOOTSTEPS. Heavy, synchronized. ECHOING from the far end of the corridor.

KAGE (40s, impeccably dressed, cold eyes) steps into view. Behind him, three ELITE SQUAD members, armed with assault rifles, move with practiced precision.

Kage’s gaze locks onto Alex, a predator recognizing its prey.

KAGE: You think you’re clever, Alex? Coming into my home... taking what isn’t yours.

Alex raises his pistol, sights Kage.

ALEX: Your home? This isn’t a home, Kage. It’s a cage. And what I took… it belongs to everyone.

KAGE: (A cold laugh) Such idealism. Always your weakness. Put it down, boy. You’re outnumbered. Outgunned.

Alex shifts his weight, eyes darting. He sees a high ventilation shaft. A long shot.

ALEX: You think so? Maybe... maybe I just like a challenge.

Kage nods to his squad. They raise their weapons.

KAGE: Take him. Alive, if possible. I want that drive. And I want him to *tell* me everything.

Alex FIRES. A quick, precise shot that takes out one of the corridor lights, plunging that section into momentary shadow.

He dives, scrambles, and with a desperate leap, catches the edge of the ventilation shaft. Pulls himself up with a GRUNT.

The ELITE SQUAD members open fire, bullets SPRAYING where Alex was a second ago, chewing into the steel door.

KAGE: (Voice echoing with rage) Don’t let him escape! He can’t hide in these mountains! Not from me!

INT. VENTILATION SHAFT - CONTINUOUS

Alex crawls, pushing hard. The metal GRINDS under his weight. He can hear the distant GUNFIRE, the shouting. He’s running out of time. He sees a grate ahead, leading outside.

EXT. MOUNTAIN CLIFF FACE - CONTINUOUS

Alex KICKS the grate open. It clangs against the rock. He drops out onto a narrow ledge, hundreds of feet above a swirling river.

The cold mountain air hits him. He glances back.

Kage and his squad emerge from a service hatch in the cliff face, appearing like ghosts. They are already spread out, forming a deadly crescent.

KAGE: (Screaming, his voice amplified by the wind) You cannot run, Alex! These mountains… they are *mine*! They will break you!

Alex stares at Kage, then at the sheer drop. He takes a deep breath, the crisp air stinging his lungs.

ALEX: (Shouting back, defiant) We'll see about that!

He turns, and without hesitation, LAUNCHES himself off the cliff edge into the terrifying abyss below.

Kage rushes to the edge, looking down. Nothing but swirling mist and the roar of the river.

Kage's face is a mask of pure fury.

KAGE: Find him! NOW! I want every rock, every crevice… TURNED OVER! I want that drive! And I want his head! GO!

The Elite Squad scrambles, rappelling gear already deploying, disappearing into the mist. Kage remains, staring into the void, his chest heaving. The sun rises higher, but the morning feels cold.

specifications.goal: Alex must retrieve a critical data drive containing incriminating evidence against Kage's organization and escape the complex, while Kage and his elite squad systematically hunt him down, aiming to capture or eliminate him.

guest.script:
INT. ABANDONED WAREHOUSE - NIGHT

RAIN drums relentlessly against the corrugated iron roof. A single, flickering fluorescent light hums overhead, casting long, distorted shadows.

DETECTIVE CORBIN (40s, worn, eyes sharp) moves with a practiced quietness. His breath mists in the cold air. He holds a GLOCK 19, muzzle pointed down.

SOUND of a creaking floorboard

Corbin freezes. Head cocked, listening.

A faint SCRAPE. From deeper within the shadows.

CORBIN
(Quietly)
Thorne. This ends tonight.

No answer. Only the rain, the hum, and the distant city THUM.

Corbin moves, his heavy boots crunching on dust and broken concrete. He scans the rows of rusting machinery, draped in cobwebs like ancient, skeletal beasts.

CLOSE ON – A pair of scuffed LEATHER BOOTS peeking out from behind a stack of corroded oil drums.

Corbin raises his weapon, moves in.

He rounds the drums.

The boots are empty. A crude MANNEQUIN head on a stick.

Corbin curses under his breath, a low GROWL.

Suddenly, a CRASH from above. Metal scraping.

Corbin looks up.

JULIAN THORNE (30s, lean, panicked but agile) is scrambling across an overhead GANTRY, a maze of pipes and catwalks. His hand-wound is visible, bleeding through a makeshift bandage, slick with rain.

CORBIN
(Shouting up)
Thorne! Stop! It's over! Don't make this worse!

Thorne doesn't look back. He moves with a desperate speed, a half-limp in his stride. He kicks open a high access hatch.

EXT. WAREHOUSE ROOF - NIGHT

Thorne bursts through the hatch, spilling onto the slick, rain-swept roof. Wind whips his hair. Below, the city sprawls, a chaotic tapestry of flickering lights.

He glances back. Corbin is already emerging from the hatch, gun up, silhouetted against the dim interior light.

Thorne makes a desperate dash across the uneven roof tiles. The wind and rain make every step treacherous. He slips, nearly goes down, but catches himself on a rusted vent pipe.

Corbin follows, slower, more methodical, but closing the distance. His uniform darkens with rain.

CORBIN
Thorne! Just stop! We can... we can talk this through! No one needs to get hurt.

Thorne doesn't respond, just pushes harder. His breath comes in ragged gasps. He spots a lower section of the roof, a steep incline leading to a brick parapet.

He slides down the incline, kicking up loose debris. Lands hard against the parapet.

Corbin reaches the top of the incline. He draws a bead on Thorne, but holds fire.

CORBIN
Don't be a fool, Thorne! You're cornered!

Thorne peers over the parapet. A sheer drop to a grimy alleyway below. Too far to jump.

He scans frantically. His eyes lock onto a thick POWER CABLE stretching across the alley to the next building. It hums faintly.

Corbin is only a few yards away, advancing slowly.

CORBIN
Put your hands up, Thorne. Now.

Thorne ignores him. He grabs the cable. It's thick, insulated, but slick with rain. He swings his leg over, straddling it like a tightrope.

THORNE
(Eyes wide, a desperate, almost manic grin)
Watch me.

He starts to pull himself, hand over hand, across the cable. Rain lashes his face, mixes with the blood from his hand.

Corbin reaches the parapet edge. He lowers his gun slightly, seeing the danger, but his eyes are locked on Thorne.

CORBIN
Thorne! Think about it! One slip, man! You'll be fried! Or dead!

Thorne ignores him, focusing on his grip. He's halfway across. His injured hand trembles, a muscle in his jaw twitching.

Suddenly, a loud ZAP! A shower of SPARKS from the cable near Thorne's hand. The HUM intensifies.

Thorne yelps, almost losing his grip. He looks back at Corbin, pure fear in his eyes, momentarily frozen.

CORBIN
(Yelling over the wind and rain)
That's live, you idiot! Get off! Now!

Thorne hesitates. He can almost see his rendezvous point, just two blocks from the other side. He can practically taste freedom.

He looks at Corbin, then back at the sparking cable. The distance to the other building suddenly feels insurmountable, the risk too great.

THORNE
(Voice strained, a desperate plea)
I... I can't. Not... not like this.

Corbin holsters his weapon. He approaches the edge, carefully, extending a hand, palm open.

CORBIN
Just get back here. I'll help you. No tricks, I swear. Just... surrender. Let's end this before someone really gets hurt.

Thorne's eyes dart between Corbin's outstretched hand and the sparking cable. The rain intensifies, plastering his hair to his forehead.

THORNE
(A whisper, barely audible over the storm)
They'll... they'll bury me. For good.

CORBIN
That's not how this works. You're not thinking straight. We get you a lawyer. We follow procedure. You know that.

Thorne looks down at the alley. His muscles ache. The cable vibrates violently beneath him.

He lets go with one hand, tries to wipe the rain from his eyes. His gaze falls on something below.

In the alley, a small, battered DELIVERY VAN is pulling away from a dumpster. The back doors are slightly ajar, revealing a dark interior.

A flicker of recognition in Thorne's eyes. A sudden, cold clarity.

THORNE
(More to himself, a grim realization)
Procedure... right.

He looks up at Corbin. A strange, knowing smile spreads across his face, not one of relief, but of bitter understanding.

THORNE
You never really wanted to catch me, did you, Corbin? Not truly. You just... needed to try. For show.

Corbin frowns, caught off guard by the shift in Thorne's demeanor.

CORBIN
What the hell are you talking about? Thorne, this is your last chance!

Thorne tightens his grip. He takes a deep, ragged breath.

THORNE
(Yelling over the wind, voice raw)
Tell Lena... I'm sorry!

And with a sudden, violent twist, Thorne lets go of the cable.

He falls.

Corbin lunges forward, a desperate hand reaching out, but Thorne is already gone, swallowed by the darkness and the rain.

Corbin stares down into the blackness of the alley.

A distant THUD, quickly masked by the sound of the van's ENGINE ROARING to life.

Corbin rushes to the edge, peering down.

The delivery van is GONE. The alley is empty, save for the rain.

Corbin slams his fist on the wet roof, a CRACK of thunder mirroring his frustration. The rain washes over him, mixing with the sweat on his face.

He pulls out his radio, hand shaking slightly.

CORBIN
(Into radio, voice tight, strained)
This is Corbin. Suspect is Julian Thorne. Last seen... (he trails off, looking at the empty alley, then across the void at the other building) ...gone. Heading south, probably. Or north. I need units. Grid search. Every damn block. And check industrial vans. Grey, old model.

He looks across the void, at the other building, then back at the empty, still-sparking cable. He knows Thorne wasn't trying to make it across. He was waiting. For something. Or someone.

The rain continues to fall, relentless.

FADE OUT.

specifications.goal: Detective Corbin's goal is to apprehend Julian Thorne, who is a prime suspect in a series of violent bank robberies, before he can disappear into the city's labyrinthine anonymity. Thorne's goal is to evade capture at all costs, believing he can escape justice if he just makes it to a pre-arranged rendezvous point.

guest.script:
INT. ABANDONED STEEL MILL - NIGHT

Rain lashes against the vast, corrugated iron roof. The sound is deafening, a relentless drumbeat against the silence of the massive, derelict space. Rusting machinery looms like skeletal giants, shadows stretching long and distorted in the beam of a FLASHLIGHT.

DETECTIVE CORBIN (40s, lean, sharp eyes that miss nothing) moves with purpose, his movements fluid, efficient. His breath plumes in the cold air. He holds his service pistol steady, raised.

He sweeps the light across an empty foreman's office, broken windows, scattered papers. Dust motes dance in the beam.

A SCRAPE of metal. Distant, almost swallowed by the rain.

Corbin stops, holds his breath. His eyes narrow, scanning the darkness beyond his light.

He moves towards the sound, footsteps careful on the slick concrete. Past a giant, rusted conveyor belt, its teeth like a prehistoric beast.

He rounds a stack of metal barrels.

VANCE (30s, athletic but visibly shaken, dark hair plastered to his forehead) is here. Hunched over a small, smoldering fire in an old drum, trying to dry a small, waterproofed TABLET. He doesn't see Corbin yet. His face is etched with a desperate focus.

Corbin raises his weapon, clicks off the safety. The sound is barely audible over the rain, but Vance's head snaps up. His eyes, wide with raw panic, lock onto Corbin.

VANCE: (Voice hoarse) No.

Corbin doesn't waver.

CORBIN: Vance. It's over.

Vance scrambles back, knocking over the drum. Embers scatter across the floor, briefly illuminating the grime. He clutches the tablet to his chest like a lifeline.

VANCE: No, it's not. Not yet.

CORBIN: You've got nowhere to go. Give me the tablet.

Vance shakes his head, a wild look in his eyes.

VANCE: I can't. You don't understand...

CORBIN: I understand everything, Vance. I understand you killed a man. I understand you've been running for three days. And I understand that tablet has everything on it.

Vance flinches at "killed a man." His gaze flickers to the tablet, then back to Corbin. A flicker of something else – fear, yes, but also a strange resolve.

VANCE: He wasn't supposed to die. It was an accident.

CORBIN: Accidents leave fingerprints, Vance. And they don't usually involve you wiping a hard drive clean. This tablet… this is the last piece.

Corbin takes a step closer. Vance retreats further, pressing himself against a cold, grimy wall.

VANCE: You don't know what's on it. What it means.

CORBIN: I know it implicates you. And whoever else you're protecting. Is that what this is? You trying to cover for someone?

Vance's jaw tightens. He looks away, a muscle twitching in his cheek.

VANCE: Just… leave me alone. Let me go.

CORBIN: Can't do that. Not with what you know. Not with what you have.

Vance's eyes dart around the space, calculating, desperate. He spots a narrow gap between two colossal, defunct presses. A possible escape route.

CORBIN: Don't. You're bleeding, Vance. You won't make it far.

Corbin points to a dark stain on Vance's torn sleeve. Vance barely registers it.

VANCE: I have to.

CORBIN: Think about this. You cooperate now, it goes better for you. We can talk about mitigating circumstances.

Vance lets out a short, humorless laugh.

VANCE: Mitigating circumstances? With *this*? You really think they'll care about circumstances when they see what's on here?

He holds up the tablet, just for a second. The screen is dark, but the gesture is defiant.

Corbin sees it. The way Vance holds it, the desperation. It's not just about his own neck.

CORBIN: Who is it, Vance? Who are you protecting? Someone you care about more than your own life, huh? That's what this is.

Vance's eyes snap back to Corbin, a raw intensity in them. Corbin has hit a nerve.

VANCE: (Low, strained) You don't understand. If this gets out… it’ll destroy everything. Not just for me. For her.

Corbin tightens his grip on his pistol. "Her."

CORBIN: Her. So there is someone else. Tell me, Vance. Help yourself.

Vance shakes his head slowly, a tear mixing with the rain on his cheek.

VANCE: I can't. I can’t.

Suddenly, Vance ducks low, making a break for the gap between the presses. A burst of unexpected speed.

CORBIN: VANCE!

Corbin moves instantly, sprinting after him. The heavy thud of his boots echoing against the rain.

Vance is surprisingly agile, squeezing through the narrow opening. Corbin follows, contorting his body to fit.

They emerge into a labyrinth of pipes and narrow catwalks. The rain is still pounding.

Vance clambers up a rickety metal ladder, two steps at a time. The ladder groans under his weight.

Corbin is right behind him, pistol still aimed.

CORBIN: Stop, Vance! It's a dead end!

Vance reaches the top, a narrow catwalk overlooking a seventy-foot drop into a murky, flooded pit. He turns, cornered, the tablet still clutched to his chest.

His eyes are wide, breathing ragged. He looks down at the pit, then at Corbin.

VANCE: You don't get it. You can't.

Corbin raises his gun higher, aiming center mass.

CORBIN: Drop the tablet, Vance. Drop it now.

Vance's gaze drops to the tablet. His fingers tighten around it. He looks at Corbin, a terrible, desperate resolve hardening his face.

VANCE: I won't let you.

With a sudden, violent movement, Vance pulls back his arm.

CORBIN: NO!

Corbin fires.

The SHOT rings out, deafening, echoing through the vast space.

Vance cries out, a choked gasp, stumbling. He doesn't fall. Not yet.

His arm, still holding the tablet, swings forward.

The tablet flies from his grasp, arcing through the air.

It plunges towards the murky depths of the flooded pit below.

Corbin lunges forward, a desperate reach, but it’s too late.

The tablet HITS the water with a soft SPLASH, disappearing beneath the surface.

Vance sags against the railing, clutching his side. Blood seeps between his fingers. His eyes, still fixed on where the tablet vanished, are full of a hollow, defeated triumph.

Corbin stares, pistol still extended, at the ripples in the dark water. The evidence. Gone.

He looks up at Vance, whose face is now a mask of pain and exhaustion, but also a strange, quiet peace.

The rain continues to fall.

FADE TO BLACK.

specifications.goal: Detective Corbin's goal is to capture Vance to retrieve critical evidence linking him to a recent murder and prevent his escape. Vance's goal is to evade capture at any cost, possibly to destroy the evidence or protect a loved one involved in the crime.

guest.script:
EXT. NORTH ATLANTIC - NIGHT

Black water. Infinite and heavy. 

A single, pinpoint light cuts through the silt—the TITAN-X SUBMERSIBLE. It looks like a toy against the crushing weight of the abyss.

INT. TITAN-X - CONTINUOUS

The space is tiny. Claustrophobic. The air is thick with the smell of recycled oxygen and unwashed skin.

MANU (40s, exhausted) stares at a flicker on the green sonar sweep. A steady *ping* echoes through the hull.

MOMO (30s, sharp) hunches over a tablet, her face lit by the cold blue glow of the UI.

MOMO
(Whispering)
It’s not shifting, Manu. The signature... it’s solid. But it’s not steel.

MANU
Check the gain. Maybe it's a ghost return from the debris field.

MOMO
I’ve checked it twice. It’s dense. It’s sitting right on the lip of the stern’s canyon.

JERRY (50s, wearing high-end tactical gear that’s never seen a day of work) leans forward, encroaching on their space.

JERRY
Is that it? Is that the cabin?

MANU
We don’t know yet, Jerry. Sit back. You’re tilting the trim.

JERRY
I didn’t pay four million for "we don't know." My researchers were clear. The private quarters of the Straus suite... they drifted. They’re down there.

RIDHS (20s, Jerry’s sister) sits in the corner, knees hugged to her chest. She stares at the small, thick acrylic viewport.

RIDHS
It’s too quiet out there.

MOMO
(To Manu)
Power’s at forty percent. If we push further into the debris shadow, we’re cutting into our ascent reserve.

JERRY
We’re not going up. Not until we see it.

MANU
(Rubbing his eyes)
Jerry, look... the pressure down here, it’s... it’s four hundred atmospheres. The hull is groaning. Do you hear that?

A sharp *CRACK* echoes—like a gunshot. Everyone freezes.

MOMO
Carbon fiber settling. Just... just the weave finding its seat.

MANU
(Low)
Or failing.

Jerry points a trembling finger at the sonar.

JERRY
There. See? That shape. That’s a door. Or a hatch.

MOMO
Wait. Manu... the sonar signature. It just... changed.

MANU
Changed how?

MOMO
It’s... pulsating. Like a rhythm.

Manu leans in. The green blip isn't a static dot anymore. It’s expanding and contracting. Softly. 

MANU
That’s impossible. It’s a rock. Or a piece of the hull. 

MOMO
(Voice trembling)
Rocks don't have a heartbeat, Manu.

RIDHS
(Suddenly)
Did you hear that?

MANU
Hear what, Ridhs?

RIDHS
Someone... someone tapped. On the outside.

A beat of silence. Only the hum of the scrubbers.

JERRY
Don’t be ridiculous. We’re twelve thousand feet down. There’s nothing out there but silt and... and rust.

*THUMP.*

A heavy, dull thud vibrates through the floorboards. 

Manu grabs the joystick. He kills the external floods. Total darkness.

MOMO
What are you doing?

MANU
Shh. Listen.

In the dark, the sounds of the deep become visceral. The groan of the metal. The trickle of a tiny, microscopic leak somewhere behind the panels.

And then—a scraping sound. Like a fingernail on a chalkboard, dragging across the exterior hull.

RIDHS
(Hyperventilating)
It’s them. They’re still down here.

JERRY
Shut up, Ridhs. Manu, turn the lights back on. Now.

MANU
Something is out there. 

MOMO
Manu, the power! It’s dropping! Thirty-five... thirty... it’s draining!

MANU
I’m not drawing it!

JERRY
(Grabbing Manu’s shoulder)
Turn. The lights. On. I want to see what I’m paying for!

Manu shoves Jerry’s hand off and flips the toggle.

The EXTERNAL FLOODS kick in.

The light hits the silt. Swirling snow of the deep. And then, the object.

It’s a section of the Titanic’s stern. Twisted, jagged. But it’s covered in something. A thick, pale organic matter. Like a web. Or skin.

And in the center of the web—a human hand.

Pressed against the outside of their viewport. 

The skin is translucent. Grey. The fingers are impossibly long.

RIDHS
(A stifled scream)
Oh God.

The hand moves. It drags down the acrylic, leaving a trail of black slime.

JERRY
(Breathless)
That... that’s not a survivor. That’s...

MOMO
Manu, the sonar! It’s not one signature anymore!

The screen erupts. Dozens of pulsating dots appear. All around them. Closing in.

MANU
(Scrambling)
Dropping the weights! We’re surfacing! Now!

He pulls the emergency release. 

Nothing happens.

MANU
The magnets... they’re not releasing.

MOMO
The power... it’s at ten percent. Something is... something is eating the charge.

The hand on the window is joined by another. Then another. Pale, sightless faces begin to press against the thick glass. They aren't ghosts. They are *evolved*.

JERRY
(Backing away)
Do something! You’re the Captain! Use the thrusters!

MANU
There’s no juice, Jerry! They’re... they’re holding us down!

A face presses against the center of the viewport. No eyes. Just pits. It opens its mouth. A row of needle-teeth.

It taps. *Tink. Tink. Tink.*

RIDHS
(Softly)
It’s the same rhythm. 

MOMO
Manu... look at the hull integrity.

A spiderweb crack appears on the internal layer of the acrylic. 

MANU
(Voice cracking)
Everyone... get to the center. Away from the glass.

JERRY
I’ll give you more. Ten million. Just... just get us up!

MANU
(Looking at the crack)
Jerry... there’s no "up" left.

The crack spreads. A high-pitched whistle starts. The sound of the ocean, waiting to get in.

The things outside stop tapping. They all lean in, watching. Waiting for the bubble to burst.

MANU
(To Momo, quiet)
I'm sorry.

MOMO
(Taking his hand)
I know.

The glass SHATTERS.

BLACKOUT.

The sound of RUSHING WATER that cuts off instantly.

FADE OUT.

specifications.goal: To locate a newly detected, anomalous sonar signature believed to be a previously undisturbed section of the Titanic's stern, potentially holding unprecedented artifacts or even human remains, while battling extreme environmental pressures, dwindling power, and rising personal conflicts within the cramped confines of the submersible.

guest.script:
EXT. CLUB की पिछली गली - रात

चमकदार क्लब की धुनें अभी भी दूर से सुनाई दे रही हैं। गली में कूड़ेदानों और खाली बोतलों का ढेर है। हवा में थोड़ी ठंडक और परफ्यूम की अजीब सी मिली-जुली महक है।

मनु (20s), जिसकी हील्स हाथ में हैं, लड़खड़ाती हुई चल रही है। उसके बाल बिखरे हुए हैं। मोमो (20s), जिसने अपनी साड़ी किसी तरह संभाल रखी है, उसके ठीक पीछे है, फोन पर कुछ ढूँढ रही है।

MOMO
(परेशान होकर)
यार, मेरा Uber क्यों नहीं आ रहा? और यहाँ नेटवर्क भी नहीं है।
(ऊपर देखती है)
और चाँद भी कहीं गायब है। मतलब, क्या प्लान है यूनिवर्स का?

MANU
(ऊँची आवाज़ में, आधी चिढ़ते हुए)
प्लान ये है कि तू चुपचाप चल और मेरे सिरदर्द में इज़ाफ़ा मत कर। हमने पार्टी करनी थी, गैंगस्टर से बचकर थोड़ी भागना था।

MOMO
(रुककर, आँखों में डर)
गैंगस्टर? कहाँ है गैंगस्टर?

MANU
(उसकी तरफ़ घूरकर)
अरे, मैंने कहा कि 'गैंगस्टर से बचकर थोड़ी भागना था'। मतलब, ऐसी सिचुएशन नहीं होनी चाहिए थी।

मोमो गहरी साँस लेती है, फिर अचानक रुक जाती है।

MOMO
(काँपती आवाज़ में)
मनु... मुझे ना... लग रहा है...

MANU
(आँखें घुमाकर)
क्या लग रहा है? कि तूने ज़्यादा ड्रिंक कर ली? या तेरी हील टूट गई? मैंने कहा था ना, वो वाले सैंडल पहन ले।

MOMO
(अपनी उंगली से सामने इशारा करती है)
नहीं। मुझे लग रहा है... वो वहाँ है।

SILAS
(धीमी, लेकिन जानलेवा आवाज़ में)
तुम शायद नहीं जानती। लेकिन तुम्हारी दोस्त... शायद जानती हो।
(मोमो की तरफ़ बढ़ता है)

MANU
(बीच में आती है, अपने हाथ फैलाकर)
नहीं! उसे कुछ मत कीजिए! वो नहीं जानती! प्लीज़!

Silas मनु को एक हल्के धक्के से रास्ते से हटाता है। मनु लड़खड़ाती है लेकिन गिरती नहीं।

SILAS
(मोमो के सामने झुकता है, उसकी आवाज़ अब सिर्फ़ फुसफुसाहट में है)
तुम बताना चाहोगी...? कहाँ है वो लिफ़ाफ़ा?

Momo अपने सर को ना में हिलाती है, उसके होंठ काँप रहे हैं, कोई आवाज़ नहीं निकल रही।

MANU
(पीछे से)
उसे नहीं पता, मैं कह रही हूँ! हमें जाने दीजिए!

Silas सीधा खड़ा होता है। उसके चेहरे पर कोई भाव नहीं है।

SILAS
तुम्हारे पास पाँच मिनट हैं। सोचो। फिर से सोचो।
(अपनी जेब से एक छोटा सा मेटल का डिब्बा निकालता है, उसे खोलता है। अंदर से कुछ छोटी, चमकीली चीज़ें दिखती हैं।)
ये दर्द... उन कागज़ात से ज़्यादा नहीं होगा।

Momo की नज़रें उस डिब्बे पर पड़ती हैं। उसकी चीख़ निकल जाती है। मनु उसे देखकर घबरा जाती है।

MANU
(चीख़ते हुए)
नहीं! ये क्या कर रहे हैं आप?! हम... हम क्या करेंगे?

Silas मुड़ता है, अँधेरे में घुल जाता है। उसकी आवाज़ गूँजती है।

SILAS (O.S.)
पाँच मिनट। घड़ी चल रही है।

मनु भागकर मोमो के पास जाती है, उसे कसकर गले लगाती है। मोमो रो रही है, उसका शरीर बुरी तरह काँप रहा है।

MOMO
(सिसकते हुए)
मनु... मैं... मुझे नहीं पता... हमें क्या करना चाहिए?

MANU
(उसका चेहरा ऊपर उठाती है)
शांत हो जा। हमें कुछ सोचना होगा।
(उसकी नज़रें वेयरहाउस के अँधेरे कोनों में भाग रही हैं। उसकी आँखों में डर है, लेकिन एक चिंगारी है – लड़ने की।)
हमें यहाँ से निकलना होगा। किसी भी हाल में।

Momo मनु की आँखों में देखती है। उसमें डर है, लेकिन मनु की मज़बूती उसे कुछ हिम्मत देती है।

एक तेज़ हवा का झोंका वेयरहाउस में आता है, दरवाज़ों और खिड़कियों से टकराता हुआ, एक डरावनी आवाज़ पैदा करता है।

FADE TO BLACK.

specifications.goal: Chloe and Maya's goal is to escape Silas unharmed and understand why they've been targeted. Silas's goal is to retrieve a specific item or piece of information he believes one of them accidentally picked up, or to deliver a chilling message for someone else. Stakes: The girls' lives and freedom are at risk, along with the unknown consequences of whatever Silas is seeking.

guest.script:
EXT. SEEDY ALLEYWAY - NIGHT

The air bites. STEAM curls from an open manhole cover, illuminating a narrow alleyway choked with overflowing dumpsters and graffiti. Rain from earlier has left puddles reflecting the distant neon glow of the city.

MANU (30s, sharp, but currently just cold) pulls his thin jacket tighter, shoulders hunched. Beside him, MOMO (30s, observant, a little less naive) shivers, her breath fogging.

They’re walking fast, trying to cut through to the main street. The alley feels longer tonight.

A SHADOW DETACHES itself from the deeper darkness ahead. Tall, imposing.

Momo instinctively grabs Manu’s arm. He stops dead.

The figure steps into the faint light. RAMESH (40s, impeccably dressed in a dark, expensive overcoat, a cold glint in his eyes). He holds nothing, but his presence is a weapon.

Ramesh offers a thin, almost polite smile.

RAMESH: Manu. Momo. Lovely evening, isn’t it? For… reflection.

Manu’s eyes dart around, assessing. No easy escape.

MANU: Ramesh. What is this? We’re just trying to get home.

Ramesh’s smile doesn’t falter, but his eyes harden.

RAMESH: Home. Yes. A concept… very dear. Very fragile, wouldn’t you say? Especially when certain… arrangements… are not honored.

Momo squeezes Manu’s arm again, tighter this time. Her gaze is fixed on Ramesh’s unnervingly still face.

MOMO: We don’t… we don’t have anything. Just a few bucks, maybe. Look, take it. Please.

She starts fumbling in her small purse.

Ramesh raises a hand, a gesture of almost gentle refusal.

RAMESH: (Softly) Oh, Momo. Please. You insult me. Do I look like a common street thief? My needs are… more refined.

He steps closer. The air drops a few degrees.

RAMESH: The item, Manu. The one you were entrusted with. The one that, by extension, *I* was entrusted with. Where is it?

Manu’s jaw tightens. He glances at Momo, then back at Ramesh.

MANU: I don’t know what you’re talking about. There’s no item. I don’t have anything of yours.

Ramesh takes another slow step. He’s close enough now to see the intricate stitching on his coat.

RAMESH: No? And yet… the whispers. They say otherwise. They say a man of your… resourcefulness… might try to keep something. Something valuable. Something that could, shall we say, alter the balance.

He leans in slightly, his voice dropping to a near whisper.

RAMESH: Tell me, Manu. Do you enjoy the quiet nights? The warm bed? The simple… predictability… of life?

Momo makes a small, choked sound.

MOMO: Manu…

Manu holds her arm, a silent command. He stares Ramesh down.

MANU: I told you. There’s nothing. Maybe you have the wrong person.

Ramesh shakes his head, a slow, pitying movement.

RAMESH: Oh, I assure you, I never have the wrong person. Mistakes… are not a luxury I afford myself. Or those in my employ. And you, Manu… you are currently very much in my employ. Indirectly, of course.

He reaches into his coat, slowly, deliberately. Manu tenses, ready to spring. Momo lets out a gasp.

Ramesh pulls out not a weapon, but a small, polished AGATE STONE. He turns it over in his fingers, admiring the deep, intricate bands of color.

RAMESH: Beautiful, isn't it? Such a small thing. So much… history. So much… power. And so very easy to lose. To misplace. To shatter.

He looks up from the stone, his eyes locking with Manu’s. The stone suddenly feels like a symbol, a threat.

RAMESH: I need the *real* one. By morning. Or the predictability of your life… will become very, very unpredictable. Starting with… the people you care about.

He nods subtly towards Momo. Her face drains of color.

MOMO: (Voice barely a whisper) What is he talking about, Manu?

Manu doesn’t answer her. His gaze is still fixed on Ramesh. A cold, hard realization dawns in his eyes. This isn't just about an item. It's about control.

MANU: You… you’ve been watching us.

Ramesh merely smiles, a hint of something genuinely chilling in his expression.

RAMESH: Always. Have a pleasant walk home, Manu. Don’t take too many detours. The night… is full of them.

He slips the agate stone back into his pocket, turns, and melts back into the alley's shadows as silently as he appeared.

Manu stands frozen, holding Momo tight. The cold wind whips through the alley, but it’s not the weather that makes them shiver.

MOMO: (Voice trembling) Manu… what was that? What does he want?

Manu slowly turns his head, looking down the alley where Ramesh disappeared. His eyes are wide, haunted.

MANU: (Barely audible) It's… it's not what he wants, Momo. It's what he *knows*. And what he's willing to do.

He pulls her close, his gaze distant, already seeing the deeper, darker plot beginning to unfold around them.

FADE TO BLACK.

specifications.goal: For Maya and Chloe to survive the unexpected encounter and understand the gangster's true, unsettling objective, which is not a simple mugging but a targeted message or demand that links back to a deeper, more dangerous plot. The gangster's goal is to instill fear and deliver a chilling warning or retrieve a specific item, establishing his power and influence.

guest.script:
EXT. ROOFTOP CAFE - NIGHT

도시의 소음이 아득하게 들린다. 난간 너머는 낭떠러지 같은 어둠이다.
테이블 위, 식어버린 커피 위로 차가운 밤바람이 스친다.

CHLOE (23)의 손등등이 미세하게 떨리고 있다. 그녀는 눈을 떼지 못한 채 허공을 응시한다.

CHLOE
그 영화... 마지막 장면 기억나? 여자가 자기 손으로 자기 목을 조르는 거.

MAYA (23)는 대답 대신 담배를 입에 문다. 불을 붙이는 손길이 거칠다.

MAYA
그거 그냥 영화야, 클로이.

CHLOE
(말을 자르며) 아니, 아니야. 그건 비명이 아니었어. 그건... 자기가 저지른 일등에 대한 유일한 속죄였어. 숨이 막혀 죽어가는 그 순간에야 비로소 살아있다는 걸 느낀 거라고.

MAYA
(연기를 내뱉으며) 미쳤네. 넌 그게 예술로 보여? 난 그냥 자살로 보여. 구역질 나는 도피일 뿐이라고.

CHLOE
(서서히 마야를 향해 고개를 돌린다) 넌 모를 거야. 네가 죽인 그 사람의 눈빛이 네 꿈속에 매일 밤 나타나는 기분이 어떤 건지.

정적. 마야의 담배 연기가 허공에서 흩어진다.

MAYA
...너 지금 무슨 소리 하는 거야?

CHLOE
영화 속 여자가 그랬지. '비밀은 무덤까지 가져가는 게 아니라, 무덤을 만드는 거라고.' 

클로이가 테이블 너머로 몸을 굽힌다. 눈이 기괴할 정도로 번뜩인다.

CHLOE
마야. 그날 밤, 비 내리던 도로에서 네가 핸들 꺾었을 때... 나 다 봤어.

마야의 얼굴에서 핏기가 가신다. 담배를 쥔 손이 굳는다.

MAYA
(낮고 날카롭게) 입 닥쳐. 

CHLOE
영화랑은 다르더라. 현실에선 슬로우 모션 같은 건 없었어. 그냥 둔탁한 소리, 그리고 네 비명뿐이었지. 근데 넌 어제 나랑 그 영화 보면서 웃더라? 어떻게 그럴 수 있어?

MAYA
(의자를 뒤로 밀며 일어선다) 그만하라고 했지. 너도 거기 같이 있었어. 너도 방조범이야, 이 미친년아!

CHLOE
(비웃듯) 그래, 나도 죽어가고 있어. 그 영화 주인공처럼. 매일 숨이 막혀서, 목을 메는 기분으로 버티고 있다고! 

클로이가 갑자기 자신의 목을 거칠게 움켜쥔다. 얼굴이 붉게 달아오른다.

MAYA
(겁에 질려) 왜 이래... 야, 클로이!

CHLOE
(쇳소리 섞인 목소리) 차라리... 그때 우리가 경찰에 신고했으면... 우리 서사가 이렇게 쓰레기 같지는 않았을 텐데. 

마야가 떨리는 손으로 가방을 움켜쥔다. 도망치듯 뒤로 물러난다.

MAYA
난 잘못 없어. 네가... 네가 가자고 한 길이었어. 다 네 탓이야.

CHLOE
(목을 놓아주며, 힘없이 웃는다) 영화 끝은 항상 이렇더라. 가장 믿었던 사람이 제일 먼저 등을 돌리는 거.

클로이가 난간 쪽으로 천천히 다가간다. 바람이 그녀의 머리카락을 사정없이 휘감는다.

MAYA
너... 거기서 뭐 해? 내려와. 당장 내려와!

CHLOE
마야, 이 영화의 장르가 뭔지 알아?

클로이가 난간 위에 위태롭게 선다. 아래는 끝을 알 수 없는 어둠뿐이다.

CHLOE
비극이야. 그리고... 주인공은 나 하나로 충분해.

MAYA
클로이!! 안 돼!!

클로이가 뒤로 쓰러지듯 몸을 던진다. 
마야의 비명소리가 옥상을 찢어놓지만, 클로이의 표정은 그 어느 때보다 평온하다.

FADE OUT.

EXT. ROOFTOP CAFE - DAY

The sun is hanging low, painting the sky in a bruised purple and orange. A light wind rustles the pages of a script on the table.

CHLOE (23, pale, eyes slightly tired) stares at the steam rising from her coffee.

CHLOE: (Inner monologue) 그 장면... 그 정적 속에서 서로를 바라보던 그 눈빛. 왜 아무 말도 하지 못했을까. 그건 단순한 침묵이 아니었는데.

MAYA (23, bright, wearing a colorful cardigan) is busy scrolling through her phone. She glances up, noticing Chloe’s distance.

CHLOE: 마야... 너 진짜 그 영화 봐야 돼. 주인공이 마지막에 기차역에서 뒤돌아보잖아? 그게 그냥 미련이 아니야. 뭐랄까, 자기 자신의 일부를 그 자리에 두고 오는 느낌?

MAYA: (Laughing softly) 또 시작이네, 영화 평론가 박클로이 선생. 기차역에서 뒤돌아보는 건 그냥... 아쉬워서 그런 거 아냐? 나처럼 배고플 때 식당 뒤돌아보는 거랑 비슷한 거지.

CHLOE: (Serious) 아니, 달라. 그건... 일종의 구원 같은 거야. 상처받을 걸 알면서도 손을 뻗는 그 비효율적인 진심. 그게 예술이 아니면 뭔데?

MAYA: 음... 너무 무거워. 난 그냥 그런 거 말고, 요즘 새로 나온 '러브 레시피' 같은 게 좋아. 주인공들이 막 엉뚱하게 엮이다가 결국엔 꽉 찬 해피엔딩인 거. 머리 안 아프고 좋잖아.

Chloe sighs, looking out at the city skyline.

CHLOE: 해피엔딩은 현실이 아니잖아. 진짜 인생은... 그 영화처럼 설명할 수 없는 틈새에 있는 건데. 넌 너무 가벼운 것만 보려고 해.

MAYA: (Sipping her drink) 가벼운 게 나쁜 건 아니지. 그리고 클로이, 너 그 영화 속 주인공의 '비효율적인 진심'은 그렇게 잘 이해하면서... 

Maya leans in, her eyes searching Chloe’s.

MAYA: 왜 네 앞집 사는 그 선배한테는 한 마디도 못 해?

Chloe freezes. She reaches for her coffee, but her hand hesitates.

CHLOE: ...그건 상황이 다르지. 이건 영화잖아.

MAYA: 아니, 똑같아. 너 어제도 복도에서 마주쳤을 때 그냥 고개만 숙이고 지나갔다며. 그게 네가 말하는 그 '깊은 서사'의 시작이야, 아니면 그냥 겁쟁이인 거야?

CHLOE: (Muttering) 그냥... 말이 안 나왔을 뿐이야. 그 공기가 너무 무거워서.

MAYA: (Teasingly) 영화였으면 거기서 슬로우 모션 걸리고 배경음악 쫙 깔렸을 텐데, 그치? 근데 현실은 그냥 정적이야. 클로이, 영화 분석은 그만하고 네 인생 장르나 좀 바꿔봐. 인디 드라마 말고, 좀 말랑말랑한 로코로.

CHLOE: 로코는 무슨... 말도 안 돼.

MAYA: 왜 안 돼? 그 선배가 너한테 빌려준 책, 아직도 안 돌려줬다며. 그거 돌려주면서 '영화 보러 갈래요?' 한 마디만 하면 되는데. 그게 네가 말하는 '예술적 모멘트'보다 훨씬 더 짜릿할걸?

Chloe looks down at the script on the table. A small, self-deprecating smile forms on her lips.

CHLOE: (Quietly) ...그 선배, 인디 영화 안 좋아할 것 같아.

MAYA: 그럼 팝콘 영화 보면 되지! 같이 웃고, 콜라 마시고. 그러다 손도 좀 스치고. 

Maya reaches over and gently taps Chloe’s hand.

MAYA: 영화는 화면 속에 있을 때가 제일 예쁘지만, 네 인생은 네가 주인공일 때가 제일 재밌는 거야. 알지?

CHLOE: (Inner monologue) 주인공... 내가? 그 서투른 뒷모습이 정말로 시작이 될 수 있을까.

Chloe looks up, the orange sunlight catching the reflection in her eyes.

CHLOE: ...그 선배한테 문자나 보내볼까. 책 다 읽었다고.

MAYA: (Grinning) 이제야 말이 좀 통하네! 자, 문구는 내가 불러줄게. '선배님, 책 너무 잘 읽었어요. 혹시 영화 취향이...'

CHLOE: (Cutting her off) 야, 그건 너무 오글거려! 내가 알아서 할게.

Chloe grabs her phone, her fingers hovering over the screen. She looks nervous, but for the first time today, she looks present.

MAYA: 오, 지금 그 표정! 방금 되게 영화 같았어. 제목은... '철벽녀의 첫 번째 메시지' 정도?

CHLOE: 진짜... 조용히 해.

Chloe starts typing. The city lights below begin to flicker on, one by one.

EXT. ROOFTOP CAFE - CONTINUOUS

The camera pulls back as the two friends lean over the phone, their laughter blending with the distant hum of the city.

FADE OUT.

specifications.goal: Chloe aims to convince Maya of the profound artistic merit of a new critically acclaimed indie drama she recently saw, hoping to inspire her friend's perspective on life and relationships. Maya's goal is to gently steer the conversation towards lighter, more escapist entertainment (like a new rom-com), while subtly trying to get Chloe to apply her cinematic insights to her own stalled love life rather than just fictional ones.

guest_test.script:
INT. THE ECHO CHAMBER - NIGHT (03:00 AM)

Low ceiling. Deep shadows. The air smells like wet pavement and ozone. 

Dozens of glass vials line the walls, pulsing with iridescent mists. They hum—a low, discordant vibration that rattles the teeth.

LYRA VALENTINE (30s) sits behind a slab of polished obsidian. She’s wearing a sharp, charcoal suit. Her eyes are too steady. Too still.

Across from her, ELIAS THORNE (40s) looks like he hasn’t slept since the turn of the decade. His hand tremors slightly as he reaches for a cigarette. He stops. Remembers the rules. Drops the hand.

ELIAS
The Petrov assassination. Three nights ago. The rooftop view.

Lyra doesn’t move. She watches the sweat bead on his forehead.

LYRA
Straight to it tonight, Elias? No... "how’s the business"? No pleasantries?

ELIAS
(Voice raspy)
The syndicate wants it back, Lyra. I need to get there before their cleaners do. Just... tell me the price.

Lyra leans forward. The light from a nearby vial casts a sickly violet glow across her cheekbones.

LYRA
I’ve got plenty of credits. My accounts are... they’re full, Elias. Overwhelmingly so.

ELIAS
Everyone has a number.

LYRA
I don't want a number. I want something... tactile. Something with weight.

She taps a fingernail against the obsidian. *Click. Click. Click.*

LYRA (CONT'D)
Your synaptic decay is getting worse. I can see the flicker in your left eye. You’re leaking, Elias.

ELIAS
I’m fine. Just... give me the memory.

LYRA
You’re holding onto one. Just one. The girl. In the yellow sundress. The park... what was it? Bluebells?

Elias stiffens. The tremor in his hand returns, sharper now. He grips the edge of the desk.

ELIAS
That’s not for sale. 

LYRA
It’s the only thing you have left that’s authentic. Everything else in that head of yours is a copy of a copy. Second-hand lives. 

ELIAS
(Softly)
It’s the only way I know I’m me.

LYRA
But you won’t be "you" in six months anyway. The decay will eat the bluebells, the dress... the way she laughed. It’ll all turn to grey static.

She slides a small, glowing data-slug across the table. The Keynote Memory.

LYRA (CONT'D)
Give it to me now, while it’s still vibrant. I’ll keep it safe. It’ll live forever in the vault. 

ELIAS
(A dry laugh)
Safe. In a jar. On a shelf.

LYRA
Better than a grave. 

Elias looks at the data-slug. Then at Lyra. He looks hollow.

ELIAS
I... I won’t remember her face. 

LYRA
You’ll remember you had a daughter. You just won’t feel the... the ache. Isn’t that what you want, Elias? To finally stop aching?

Elias reaches out. His fingers hover over the slug. He hesitates. His breathing is shallow, jagged.

ELIAS
If I do this... I’m just a ghost.

LYRA
(A thin smile)
Look at the clock, Elias. It’s three a.m. We’re all ghosts here.

Elias closes his eyes. A tear tracks through the grime on his face. He leans his head back, exposing a port at the base of his skull.

ELIAS
...Do it. Before I change my mind.

Lyra stands. She picks up a delicate, silver needle-lead. She moves with the grace of a surgeon.

LYRA
Don't blink. It’s faster if you don’t fight it.

The needle slides in. 

Elias’s body jerks. His eyes roll back, showing only the whites. 

A faint, golden mist begins to flow through the tube, leaving his head and filling an empty vial on the desk. 

In the mist, for a fraction of a second, we see a blur of yellow fabric and hear the faint, ghostly sound of a child’s giggle.

Then, the mist settles. The vial glows.

Elias slumps forward, his forehead hitting the cold stone. 

Lyra detaches the lead. She picks up the Keynote Memory and drops it in front of him. 

ELIAS
(Muffled, confused)
...Why am I here?

Lyra looks at the vial of golden mist. She looks almost... envious.

LYRA
You were buying something, Elias. 

Elias pushes himself up. He looks at the data-slug. He looks at Lyra. His eyes are clear now. Empty.

ELIAS
Right. The Petrov job. 

He pockets the slug. He stands up, his movements robotic, efficient. The tremor is gone.

ELIAS (CONT'D)
Thanks... I think.

He turns and walks toward the exit. 

LYRA
Elias?

He stops at the door. He doesn’t turn around.

ELIAS
Yeah?

LYRA
Does it feel better? The silence?

Elias stands there for a beat. The sound of the rain outside seems to grow louder, filling the void.

ELIAS
I don't know what you're talking about.

He steps out into the rain.

Lyra picks up the vial. She holds it to her ear, listening to a memory that isn't hers, looking for a feeling she'll never have.

FADE OUT.

characters.name: ELIAS THORNE
characters.traits: Cynical, weary, and suffering from 'Synaptic Decay'—a side effect of viewing too many foreign memories. He is obsessively looking for a memory of his own that he sold years ago.

characters.name: LYRA VALENTINE
characters.traits: Cold, calculated, and impeccably dressed. She operates a black-market 'Vault' and views human experiences as mere currency. She has no memories of her own, only those she has harvested.

specifications.setting: The Echo Chamber: A subterranean, rain-drenched lounge in the neon-lit underbelly of Neo-Cascadia. The walls are lined with glowing glass vials containing swirling, iridescent mists (liquid memories).
specifications.tone: Gritty, melancholic, and high-tension. The atmosphere is thick with the scent of ozone and the weight of forgotten lives.
specifications.goal: Elias must secure a 'Keynote Memory' of a high-profile assassination before it is wiped or sold to a rival syndicate. Lyra refuses to take money; she demands a trade for Elias's last remaining memory of his daughter—the only thing keeping him grounded in reality.

guest_6gh1f2mu11da9cl7ppy2iw.script:
[SCENE START]

INT. JULIAN'S PRIVATE STUDY - NIGHT

Rain lashes against the floor-to-ceiling windows overlooking the city. The room is vast, filled with leather-bound books and cold, expensive furniture. 

JULIAN (60s, sharp, tailored) sits behind a massive mahogany desk. He’s swirling a glass of amber liquid. Ice clinks.

The door SWINGS open. 

ELIAS (30s, disheveled, eyes bloodshot) stands in the doorway. He holds a single manila folder. He’s breathing hard.

JULIAN
(Without looking up)
The deadbolt was locked, Elias. I’ll have to have words with the staff.

ELIAS
I didn't use the door. I used the service elevator. The one you forgot to change the code for.

Elias walks to the desk. His hands are shaking. He drops the folder on the polished wood. It slides, stopping inches from Julian’s glass.

ELIAS (CONT'D)
Sign it.

Julian finally looks up. His face is a mask of practiced concern.

JULIAN
Sign what? It’s nearly midnight. You look... you look unwell, Elias. Truly.

ELIAS
(Voice cracking)
I look like a man who spent three days in a basement looking at bank records. Sign the confession. You forged the signatures. You took the estate. You took... everything.

Julian doesn't touch the folder. He sips his drink. Slow.

JULIAN
Forged? That’s a very... it’s a very loud word. 

ELIAS
I have the handwriting analysis. The dates don't match. Dad was in the ICU on the fourteenth. He couldn't hold a pen, let alone sign a transfer of power.

Julian sighs, a heavy, fatherly sound. He leans forward.

JULIAN
Elias... we talked about this. In the hospital. Don’t you remember?

ELIAS
Don't. Don’t do that.

JULIAN
The doctors said the stress might trigger another... well, another "gap." 

ELIAS
(A frantic laugh)
A gap? There’s no gap. I was there. I saw you coming out of his room with the lawyers.

JULIAN
(Softly)
I wasn't in the hospital on the fourteenth, Elias. I was in London. I have the flight logs.

ELIAS
(Stunned)
No. No, I... I saw you. You had that gray coat on. You were whispering to Miller.

JULIAN
Miller died six months ago. 

Elias freezes. He blinks rapidly, his mind racing to catch up.

ELIAS
No. That’s... that’s not right. I talked to him. Yesterday.

JULIAN
(Sighing)
See? This is what I’m talking about. You’re doing it again. The mixing of timelines. It’s the grief, Elias. It’s a heavy lid to carry.

Julian stands up. He walks around the desk, moving like a predator in a silk suit. He puts a hand on Elias’s shoulder. Elias flinches, but doesn't move away.

JULIAN (CONT'D)
You remember the summer at the lake? When you thought you’d lost the keys to the boat? You screamed at the gardener for hours. 

ELIAS
(Whispering)
He took them.

JULIAN
They were in your pocket, Elias. You’d put them there and forgotten. Just like you’re forgetting now. You want to believe I’m the villain because it’s easier than admitting your own mind is... fraying.

ELIAS
(Grabbing the folder)
The papers are real! The signatures... they’re fake! Look at them!

Elias shoves a paper under Julian’s nose. Julian doesn't even glance at it.

JULIAN
If I sign that... do you know what happens? The legacy dies. The company stock plunges. Thousands of people lose their jobs. All because you’re having a "moment." 

ELIAS
It’s not a moment. I... I have proof.

JULIAN
(Leaning in close)
Who will believe you? The man who spent six months in a "wellness retreat" last year? Or the man who has kept this family’s name in the black for thirty years?

ELIAS
I wasn't... it wasn't a retreat. You sent me there.

JULIAN
For your own safety. And looking at you now? With that look in your eye? I think it might be time to go back.

Elias looks at the paper. Then at Julian. The confidence in his eyes is leaking out, replaced by a terrifying void of doubt.

ELIAS
(Small voice)
I saw you, Julian. I know I did.

JULIAN
(Patting his cheek)
Memory is a traitor, Elias. It tells us what we want to hear. Go home. Sleep. We’ll talk about a doctor in the morning.

Julian picks up the folder. He doesn't open it. He walks over to the fireplace and drops it onto the glowing embers.

ELIAS
No! 

Elias lunges for the fire, but Julian catches him by the arm. His grip is like iron.

JULIAN
(Hissing)
Let it go. For the sake of the name. For your own sake.

Elias watches the paper curl. Black ink turns to gray ash. He sinks to his knees in front of the hearth.

JULIAN (CONT'D)
(Gentle again)
There. Isn't that better? The weight is gone.

Elias doesn't answer. He just stares into the fire, his reflection flickering in his wide, empty eyes.

JULIAN (CONT'D)
I’ll call the driver. You shouldn't be behind a wheel in this state.

Julian walks back to his desk. He picks up his glass. 

JULIAN (CONT'D)
And Elias? Don't use the service elevator again. It’s... well, it’s bad for your paranoia.

Elias remains on the floor. A broken man in a room full of expensive things.

[SCENE END]

specifications.goal: Elias attempts to force Julian to sign a confession regarding the forged signatures that stripped Elias of his inheritance, while Julian attempts to gaslight Elias into believing he is mentally unstable to protect the legacy.
